missables: Avenger and Rematch (Wakka's weapon) are permanently lost if you use the Trigger Command gate strategy to defeat Evrae Altana — the gates lock behind you and block both treasure chests.
sgTip: The Via Purifico is one of the best places to give Yuna substantial AP — she's been absent from your active party since Bevelle and is well behind on the grid. Avoid using Bahamut's Impulse to blitz through random encounters; let Yuna take hits and cast spells herself to build sphere levels before the Isaaru fight.

subLocations.name: Via Purifico (Yuna's Path)
subLocations.prose: The party is split and sentenced to the Via Purifico — Yuna, Kimahri, and Lulu in the labyrinth above; Tidus, Rikku, and Wakka in the underwater channels below. Yuna starts alone. Move east out of the starting chamber and follow the corridor north to find Kimahri (Mega-Potion nearby). Continue north then west to reach the Save Sphere near Auron's ghost-image — use it. Avoid teleport pads for now and head west, then south to find Lulu (White Magic Sphere). Press south to the dead end: collect the Elixir and activate the glyph in the rubble. This enables the floor glyph near the first Save Sphere. Return north and take the first right from that Save Sphere — collect the Black Magic Sphere. Step on the floor glyph to shift a teleport pad; ride it into the locked room for the Skill Sphere, Lucid Ring, and 10,000 Gil. Head north through the red-lit corridor when ready for Isaaru. Maze Larva and Aqua Flan are the primary enemies — fire and ice from Lulu handle them quickly. Wandering for AP here is valuable since Yuna has been sidelined since Bevelle.

subLocations.name: Via Purifico (Tidus's Sewers)
subLocations.prose: Tidus, Rikku, and Wakka navigate the underwater channels below Bevelle. Immediately to the right of Tidus's starting position is a blue shop chest — stock up on Phoenix Downs and items before heading deeper. The channel is short with only two or three encounters. Enemies here are almost universally weak to lightning so Lightningstrike weapons cut through them quickly. Phlegyas and Exoray are the main threats — Exoray's Spore inflicts Darkness and Confusion, so equip Confuseproof armor if available. Save at the sphere before the round chamber. Evrae Altana is undead: two Phoenix Downs deal roughly 9,999 damage each and kill it outright. Do not use cure spells or Al Bhed Potions — both will heal it. The gate strategy (using Trigger Commands to lock gates and trap Evrae in the final chamber) also works, but doing so permanently seals off the Avenger and Rematch chests further back in the channels. If you want those items, kill Evrae in place with Phoenix Downs instead.

bosses.strategy:
Three sequential aeon fights — Yuna's aeons face Isaaru's. Your aeons will always have higher stats because they've been upgraded throughout the game. Charge Overdrives before entering, especially on Bahamut.

Fight 1 — Grothia (Isaaru's Ifrit, HP 8,000): Weak to Ice. Summon Shiva and open with Diamond Dust if Overdrive is ready — otherwise use Blizzara. This is the easiest of the three fights.

Fight 2 — Pterya (Isaaru's Valefor, HP 12,000): No elemental weakness. Summon whichever of your aeons is closest to Overdrive and attack with its strongest ability. Use Energy Ray if your Valefor is available. If Pterya nears its own Overdrive bar, use Shield to reduce incoming damage.

Fight 3 — Spathi (Isaaru's Bahamut, HP 20,000): The toughest fight. After taking damage, Spathi begins a countdown — use Shield when it hits zero to halve the incoming Mega Flare damage. Summon your Bahamut if possible and fire Mega Flare first; your Bahamut's superior stats should deal heavy damage. Alternatively, use Ixion or Shiva (they can self-heal by casting their own black magic on themselves if injured). Burn Overdrives immediately and keep attacking — Spathi has no elemental resistances so any strong physical or magical hit connects.

Evrae Altana is fully afflicted with zombie status — two Phoenix Downs kill it outright, dealing roughly 9,999 damage each. Alternatively, use the Trigger Commands to open the locks on the gates behind you; escaping through all the gates traps Evrae in the final underwater chamber where your attacks deal more damage and some of its attacks are prevented. Warning: if you use the gate escape, the gates lock behind you and you permanently lose access to the Avenger and Wakka's Rematch further back in the channels.
